title: The Business of Being a 'White Monkey': How Foreigners Are Hired to Boost Brands in China
subtitle: Companies are paying people from abroad to act as fake experts and customers to improve their public image.

summary.en: In China, some businesses hire foreigners to pose as professionals or customers to gain social status. This practice is often called the 'white monkey' phenomenon. These individuals are paid to make local companies appear more international and successful. While the practice helps businesses gain 'face' or respect, it operates in a legal grey area. Critics note that these roles rely on stereotypes rather than actual skills or qualifications.
summary.zh: 在中國，有些企業聘請外國人假扮專業人士或顧客以獲取社會地位。這種做法通常被稱為「白猴子」現象。這些人受雇讓當地公司看起來更具國際化且更成功。雖然這種做法有助於企業贏得「面子」或尊重，但它處於法律的灰色地帶。評論家指出，這些角色依賴的是刻板印象，而非實際的技能或資格。

paragraphs.en: In many parts of China, businesses are using a unique strategy to attract customers and gain respect: they hire foreigners to play roles they are not actually qualified for. This practice is known as the 'white monkey' phenomenon. These individuals are paid to pose as doctors, lawyers, scientists, or even company CEOs. By simply having a person who looks like a foreigner involved in their business, these companies hope to appear more international and successful to local customers.
paragraphs.zh: 在中國許多地方，企業正利用一種獨特的策略來吸引顧客並贏得尊重：他們聘請外國人扮演他們實際上並不具備專業資格的角色。這種做法被稱為「白猴子」現象。這些人受雇假扮成醫生、律師、科學家，甚至是公司執行長。透過讓外國面孔參與業務，這些公司希望在當地顧客眼中顯得更具國際化且更成功。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian Business, this trend is rooted in the cultural concept of 'mianzi', or 'face'. In Chinese society, 'face' is about the respect and status a person or business receives from others. When a business can show that it has connections to the outside world, it gains prestige. This makes the company look more professional and trustworthy compared to its competitors. For many business owners, the 'foreign look' is a simple way to get an edge in a crowded market.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報商業版》（The Guardian Business）報導，這種趨勢源於「面子」的文化概念。在華人社會中，「面子」指的是個人或企業從他人那裡獲得的尊重與地位。當一家企業能展現出與外部世界的連結時，它就能獲得聲望。這使得該公司相較於競爭對手顯得更專業、更值得信賴。對許多企業主而言，「外國面孔」是在擁擠市場中取得優勢的簡單途徑。

paragraphs.en: These jobs cover a wide range of activities. Some people are hired to sit in restaurants to make them look popular, while others are paid to act as English teachers in language centers. The Guardian Business reports that these companies often prefer these individuals over better-qualified local teachers because parents believe that a native English speaker is automatically better. In other cases, foreigners are hired to act as judges on television talent shows or to appear in advertisements for products like blood pressure monitors. These roles are often found through informal job boards or private groups on the messaging app WeChat.
paragraphs.zh: 這些工作涵蓋了廣泛的活動。有些人受雇坐在餐廳裡，讓餐廳看起來很受歡迎；另一些人則受雇在語言中心擔任英語教師。《衛報商業版》指出，這些公司通常偏好聘請這些人，而非資歷更佳的當地教師，因為家長認為母語人士自動就比較優秀。在其他案例中，外國人被聘請擔任電視才藝節目的評審，或出現在血壓計等產品的廣告中。這些職缺通常透過非正式的求職佈告欄或通訊軟體微信（WeChat）的私人群組發布。

paragraphs.en: While the term 'white monkey' suggests that only white people are hired, the reality is more complex. Recruiters often look for anyone who appears 'non-Chinese'. The Guardian Business noted that job requests can be very specific, asking for people of different races or nationalities depending on what the product needs. For example, a company might ask for a specific type of model to make a video look more global. Because these requests are often made through private messages rather than formal job advertisements, they are very difficult for the government to control or regulate.
paragraphs.zh: 雖然「白猴子」一詞暗示只有白人會被聘用，但現實情況更為複雜。招募人員通常尋找任何看起來「非中國人」的面孔。據《衛報商業版》指出，工作需求可能非常具體，會根據產品需求要求不同種族或國籍的人士。例如，公司可能會要求特定類型的模特兒，讓影片看起來更具全球感。由於這些需求通常透過私人訊息而非正式徵才廣告發布，政府很難對其進行控管或規範。

paragraphs.en: For the people hired, these jobs can provide easy money. One person named Piers, who has worked in several of these roles, shared his experience with The Guardian Business. As a student in Shanghai in 2010, he was paid to sit on an 'international judging panel' for a television show. He and other students were sorted by their country of origin and asked to watch performances and give out toy prizes. He noted that the pay was good for an unemployed student, and it offered a chance to be on television. In another instance, he realized a restaurant had placed him in a premium seat specifically to attract attention from passersby, as locals assumed that if a foreigner was eating there, the food must be of high quality.
paragraphs.zh: 對於受雇者而言，這些工作能提供輕鬆的收入。一位名為皮爾斯（Piers）的人士曾擔任過多個此類角色，他與《衛報商業版》分享了自己的經驗。二〇一〇年，身為上海學生的他受雇在一個電視節目中擔任「國際評審團」成員。他和學生們被按國籍分組，並被要求觀看表演後發放玩具獎品。他提到，對於失業學生來說，這份薪水不錯，且提供了上電視的機會。在另一個例子中，他發現一家餐廳將他安排在顯眼的座位，專門為了吸引路人注意，因為當地人認為如果有外國人在那裡用餐，食物品質一定很好。

paragraphs.en: However, this industry exists in a legal grey area. While China has strict laws regarding equality in the workplace, these informal 'white monkey' arrangements often avoid formal contracts. This means that the people involved have very little protection if something goes wrong. Furthermore, the practice relies on the idea that foreign presence is inherently better than local expertise, which some critics argue is a misleading way to do business.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，這個產業處於法律的灰色地帶。儘管中國對職場平權有嚴格法律，但這些非正式的「白猴子」安排通常避開了正式合約。這意味著若發生問題，相關人員幾乎沒有保障。此外，這種做法依賴於「外國人存在本身就優於當地專業」的觀念，一些評論家認為這是一種誤導性的商業手段。

paragraphs.en: As the Chinese market continues to grow and change, the demand for these performative roles remains steady. Businesses continue to use these strategies to build their brand image, believing that the perception of being global is just as important as the reality. For now, as long as companies believe that a 'foreign look' can help them gain more customers and higher status, the 'white monkey' industry is likely to continue operating behind the scenes of the Chinese economy.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著中國市場持續成長與變遷，對這類表演性角色的需求依然穩定。企業持續利用這些策略來建立品牌形象，認為「具備國際感」的印象與實際情況同樣重要。目前看來，只要企業仍相信「外國面孔」能幫助他們獲得更多顧客與更高地位，「白猴子」產業很可能會繼續在中國經濟的幕後運作。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To pretend to be someone else in order to deceive people.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見動詞 pose 作「擺姿勢」解，這裡指假扮身份。文中：These individuals are paid to pose as doctors, lawyers, scientists, or even company CEOs.

keyTerms.definitionEn: An advantage over other people or things.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（邊緣），這裡指競爭優勢。文中：For many business owners, the 'foreign look' is a simple way to get an edge in a crowded market.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To arrange things or people into groups based on specific characteristics.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（整理），這裡指將人依據國籍分組。文中：He and other students were sorted by their country of origin and asked to watch performances and give out toy prizes.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A situation that is not clearly defined by rules or laws.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 灰色地帶（指法律或規定不明確的領域）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意為「灰色區域」，引申為法律模糊地帶。文中：However, this industry exists in a legal grey area.

sources.name: The Guardian Business
sources.title: Fake lawyers, scientists, chefs and punters: meet the ‘white monkeys’ paid to make Chinese businesses look global

quiz.question: According to the article, why are some foreign individuals hired to act as judges on television talent shows?

options.A: Because they have professional backgrounds in the entertainment industry.
options.B: To enhance the perceived international status and prestige of the show.
options.C: To provide expert critiques that local judges are unable to offer.
options.D: Because the government mandates that talent shows must include diverse panels.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段與第三段，企業聘請外國人是為了透過「異國面孔」來提升公司或活動的「面子」（mianzi）與國際形象，進而讓大眾覺得該企業更專業、更成功。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章提到這些人往往並不具備相關專業資格；選項 C 錯誤，文章並未提到他們提供專業意見，反而在第五段提到他們只是被分配坐著並發放玩具獎品；選項 D 錯誤，文章明確指出這些行為多透過私下管道進行，且政府難以監管，並非政府規定。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the 'white monkey' industry regarding its impact on local employees?

options.A: It creates a more competitive environment that encourages local workers to improve their skills.
options.B: It provides local workers with opportunities to collaborate with international experts.
options.C: It may disadvantage qualified local professionals by prioritizing appearance over actual expertise.
options.D: It leads to higher wages for local employees due to the increased success of the businesses.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第三段，公司常偏好聘請外國人擔任英語老師，即使這些人並不比當地老師更有資格，原因僅是家長迷信「母語人士一定比較好」。由此可推論，這種現象排擠了具備實質能力的當地專業人士。選項 A 與 D 過於樂觀，文章並未提及此現象對當地員工有正面激勵或加薪效果；選項 B 錯誤，因為文章強調這些外國人往往只是在「表演」角色，並非真正的專家，因此不存在實質的專業合作。

quiz.question: In the context of the final paragraph, what is the best meaning of the word 'performative' as used in the phrase 'demand for these performative roles'?

options.A: Designed to create a specific impression rather than to perform a real task.
options.B: Requiring advanced acting skills and professional theatrical training.
options.C: Highly effective at increasing the actual quality of a company's products.
options.D: Strictly regulated by law to ensure the safety of the workers involved.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文中提到這些人被聘請去扮演醫生、律師或在餐廳充當顧客，其目的並非真的要執行該職務，而是為了讓公司看起來「更國際化」。因此，這裡的 'performative' 指的是為了展示給人看、營造形象的行為。選項 B 錯誤，這些工作通常不需要專業演技；選項 C 錯誤，文章強調這是一種「表演」而非提升產品品質的實質手段；選項 D 錯誤，文章提到該產業處於法律灰色地帶，缺乏監管。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the central theme of the article?

options.A: The Chinese government is actively working to ban the hiring of foreigners for fake jobs.
options.B: Foreigners living in China are finding it increasingly difficult to secure legitimate employment.
options.C: The 'white monkey' phenomenon reflects a business strategy that prioritizes the appearance of global status over substance.
options.D: The 'white monkey' trend is a dying practice as local consumers become more skeptical of foreign faces.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文核心在於探討中國市場中「白猴子」現象的背景與動機，即企業利用外國人的外表來營造國際化形象以獲取「面子」與競爭優勢，即便這些人並無實質專業。選項 A 錯誤，文章提到政府難以監管；選項 B 並非文章主旨，文章聚焦於企業行為而非外國人的就業困境；選項 D 錯誤，文章結尾明確指出該產業需求依然穩健，且預期會持續運作。
